sn22.59:1.1: At one time the _Bhagavā_ was dwelling at Bārāṇasī, in the Deer Park at Isipatana.
sn22.59:1.2: There the _Bhagavā_ addressed the five *bhikkhus* of the group:
sn22.59:10.1: Whatever consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] there is, whether past, future, or present, internal or external, coarse or subtle, inferior or superior, far or near — all consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] should be seen as it truly is through proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
sn22.59:11.1: Seeing thus, *bhikkhus*, a learned noble disciple becomes disenchanted with *rūpa*, becomes disenchanted with *vedanā*, becomes disenchanted with *saññā*, becomes disenchanted with *saṅkhāras*, becomes disenchanted with consciousness [*viññāṇa*].
sn22.59:11.2: Becoming disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate; through dispassion he is liberated. When liberated, there is the knowledge: ‘Liberated.’
sn22.59:11.3: He understands: ‘Birth is ended, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.’”
sn22.59:12.2: Satisfied, the five *bhikkhus* of the group rejoiced in what the _Bhagavā_ had said.
sn22.59:12.3: And while this explanation was being spoken, the minds [*cittāni*] of the five *bhikkhus* of the group were liberated from the *āsavas* through non-clinging/feeding [*anupādāya*].
sn22.59:2.2: And if this *rūpa*, *bhikkhus*, were self, this *rūpa* would not lead to affliction, and it would be possible with regard to *rūpa*:
sn22.59:2.3: ‘Let my *rūpa* be thus; let my *rūpa* not be thus.’
sn22.59:2.4: But because *rūpa*, *bhikkhus*, is not-self [*anattā*], therefore *rūpa* leads to affliction, and it is not possible with regard to *rūpa*:
sn22.59:2.5: ‘Let my *rūpa* be thus; let my *rūpa* not be thus.’
sn22.59:3.2: And if this *vedanā*, *bhikkhus*, were self, this *vedanā* would not lead to affliction, and it would be possible with regard to *vedanā*:
sn22.59:3.3: ‘Let my *vedanā* be thus; let my *vedanā* not be thus.’
sn22.59:3.4: But because *vedanā*, *bhikkhus*, is not-self [*anattā*], therefore *vedanā* leads to affliction, and it is not possible with regard to *vedanā*:
sn22.59:3.5: ‘Let my *vedanā* be thus; let my *vedanā* not be thus.’
sn22.59:4.3: And if these *saṅkhāras*, *bhikkhus*, were self, these *saṅkhāras* would not lead to affliction, and it would be possible with regard to *saṅkhāras*:
sn22.59:4.4: ‘Let my *saṅkhāras* be thus; let my *saṅkhāras* not be thus.’
sn22.59:4.5: But because *saṅkhāras*, *bhikkhus*, are not-self [*anattā*], therefore *saṅkhāras* lead to affliction, and it is not possible with regard to *saṅkhāras*:
sn22.59:4.6: ‘Let my *saṅkhāras* be thus; let my *saṅkhāras* not be thus.’
sn22.59:5.2: And if this consciousness [*viññāṇañca*], *bhikkhus*, were self, this consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] would not lead to affliction, and it would be possible with regard to consciousness [*viññāṇe*]:
sn22.59:5.3: ‘Let my consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] be thus; let my consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] not be thus.’
sn22.59:5.4: But because consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*], *bhikkhus*, is not-self [*anattā*], therefore consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] leads to affliction, and it is not possible with regard to consciousness [*viññāṇe*]:
sn22.59:5.5: ‘Let my consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] be thus; let my consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] not be thus.’
sn22.59:6.14: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], is that *dukkha* or *sukha*?”
sn22.59:6.16: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, liable to change, is it fit to regard that as:
sn22.59:6.17: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
sn22.59:6.4: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], is that *dukkha* or *sukha*?”
sn22.59:6.6: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, liable to change, is it fit to regard that as:
sn22.59:6.7: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
sn22.59:7.1: “Therefore, *bhikkhus*, whatever *rūpa* there is, whether past, future, or present, internal or external, coarse or subtle, inferior or superior, far or near — all *rūpa* should be seen as it truly is through proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
sn22.59:8.1: Whatever *vedanā* there is, whether past, future, or present, internal or external … far or near — all *vedanā* should be seen as it truly is through proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
sn22.59:9.2: whatever *saṅkhāras* there are, whether past, future, or present, internal or external … far or near — all *saṅkhāras* should be seen as they truly are through proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
